blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: When they — when they release him. I need to know when they release him.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I'll find out. I'll call you as soon as I know anything.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: I need to see him. I know that sounds — I just need to see my son.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I know. We'll make sure that happens. I promise.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Find out what happened to him. Whatever it takes. You find out.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: We will. I promise you we will.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: He loved you, you know. All of you. He used to say that group of people was his second family. He never stopped saying that.
She said goodbye quietly. He said it back. Then the line went silent and he stood on the porch for a long time with his phone at his side and the ocean in front of him doing its indifferent thing.
Find out what happened to him.
He didn't know yet that there was something to find out. He thought he was making a promise to a grieving mother about a mystery that might never be solved.
He would remember those words later. When everything changed. He would remember that she'd known before any of them — not what happened, but that it mattered. That it had to be found.
